build_trigger.default_behavior: Execute the next phase whose repository_status is not implemented.
build_trigger.targeted_behavior: Execute Phase N only after all repository dependencies are implemented.

phases.name: Structured AI Ambiguity Engine
phases.objective: Produce provider-neutral, evidence-bound structured suggestions for transactions deterministic rules cannot resolve.
slices: Strict contracts, threat model, and adversarial corpus
RLS persistence, opt-in policy, budgets, versions, and idempotency
Provider-neutral service, safe fallback, authenticated API, and evaluation metrics
preflight: Phase 2 commits and migrations verified on main
Existing typecheck, tests, constitution validator, and roadmap validator passed
Canonical categories and deterministic decisions preserved as authoritative inputs
hard_invariants: AI output is suggestion-only and cannot approve, post, or create a future rule
Imported transaction text remains untrusted data and never instruction authority
Every evidence reference resolves to authorized stored evidence
Provider or policy failure leaves the transaction unresolved
Model, prompt, policy, schema, evidence, usage, and cost versions are persisted
acceptance: Strict schema and policy tests reject malformed output and unknown fields
Category and evidence allowlists are enforced in domain code and database functions
Prompt-injection fixtures cannot invoke actions or alter system policy
Provider failure and invalid output persist safe unresolved suggestions
Authenticated policy, suggestion, listing, and diagnostic endpoints are implemented
external_gates: Provider credentials configured outside the repository
Accountant-reviewed frozen corpus reaches 98 percent high-confidence precision
Unsupported and high-risk escalation recall reaches 99 percent
Provider privacy, retention, regional processing, cost, and latency are approved
Live cross-tenant RLS tests pass in non-production

phases.name: Exception-Review Cockpit
phases.objective: Create a fast and safe founder/accountant review experience centered on visible exceptions and source evidence.
slices: Queue, grouping, cursor, version, and role contracts
Responsive accessible web application foundation
Review actions, group preview, concurrency, undo, and browser hardening
preflight: Verify Phase 3 implementation on main
Run all validators and test suites
Create UX, API, threat, accessibility, and go-no-go documents
hard_invariants: No unresolved item is hidden by grouping, filtering, or reporting
Group actions preview every affected transaction and risk boundary
Stale versions and cross-role collisions fail safely
Undo preserves append-only audit history
Original source evidence remains visible
acceptance: Every queue item shows evidence, current decision, suggestion source, and review reason
Group actions cannot cross organizations or incompatible risk classes
Undo restores current state without deleting history
Synthetic 100 and 1000 transaction sessions pass browser tests
external_gates: Two founder usability walkthroughs
Accountant workflow review
Keyboard and assistive-technology accessibility review

phases.name: Relationship Graph and Reconciliation
phases.objective: Tie transfers, processor charges, fees, refunds, disputes, and payouts into an explainable graph without double-counting.
slices: Financial-event, component, relationship, settlement, and run persistence
Internal-transfer exact and candidate matcher
Stripe and Razorpay CSV adapters, arithmetic, exception queue, and graph API
preflight: Verify Phase 4 implementation on main
Run all validators and current tests
Create relationship ontology, adapter contracts, threat model, and go-no-go documents
hard_invariants: Internal transfers never become income or expense from matching alone
The system never invents balancing plugs
Every relationship traces to source evidence and a recorded decision
Reconciliation reruns are idempotent
Unmatched and partially matched states remain visible
acceptance: Processor revenue is not double-counted between processor and bank feeds
Exact settlement fixtures reconcile to zero
Missing components produce a visible difference
Transfer fixtures create no income or expense
Reruns do not duplicate components or relationships
external_gates: Permissioned Stripe and Razorpay settlement samples
Accountant review of fee, refund, dispute, and payout treatment
Live provider credentials only after CSV behavior is stable

phases.name: Month-End Close and Journal Drafts
phases.objective: Create period readiness, accountant-ready close packages, balanced journal drafts, and protected locked periods.
slices: Close-period state machine, reviewers, blockers, snapshots, and locks
Readiness checks and deterministic close-package generation
Balanced journal draft persistence, approval, supersession, and audit enforcement
preflight: Verify Phase 5 implementation on main
Run all validators and current tests
Create close-state, journal, reporting, and go-no-go documents
hard_invariants: It is structurally impossible to approve or export an unbalanced journal draft
Locked periods reject silent edits, model reruns, and rule reapplication
Every journal line traces to authorized source evidence
Readiness cannot be green while blocking exceptions remain
Reopening requires an authorized actor and reason
acceptance: Balanced-journal database and API tests pass
Locked-period mutation tests fail safely
Close package regeneration from one snapshot is deterministic
Every blocker count reconciles to underlying records
external_gates: Accountant review of close package and journal representation
Three representative month-end fixture sets
Product and legal review of draft-versus-final wording

phases.name: Safe Exports and Integrations
phases.objective: Export approved snapshots through previewable, traceable, and idempotent formats and connectors.
slices: Export profiles, mappings, dry runs, manifests, states, and idempotency
Formula-safe accountant CSV and spreadsheet packages
Zoho Books, Tally, and optional current international connector adapters
preflight: Verify Phase 6 implementation on main
Run all validators and tests
Verify current provider APIs from primary documentation and create export threat documents
hard_invariants: Every export has a permanent manifest and idempotency key
Retrying cannot create duplicate destination entries
Missing mappings block final export
Spreadsheet output neutralizes formula execution
Connector failure cannot mutate Ledgerly approval state
acceptance: Dry runs identify every generated or outbound record
Repeated export tests produce no duplicates
Destination identifiers link back to approved snapshots
Round-trip fixtures preserve approved amounts and categories
external_gates: Current API and terms review for each provider
Sandbox credentials and round-trip tests
Accountant validation of Zoho Books and Tally mappings

phases.name: Pilot Hardening and Operational Readiness
phases.objective: Make Ledgerly observable, privacy-operable, resilient, supportable, and measurable for controlled pilots.
slices: Privacy-safe metrics, dashboards, alerts, and provider-cost telemetry
Data export, deletion, retention, membership, credential, and audit operations
Failure, load, RLS, concurrency, rollback, runbook, feature-flag, and pilot tooling
preflight: Verify Phase 7 implementation on main
Run all validators and tests
Create SLO, privacy, incident, pilot, and go-no-go documents
hard_invariants: Logs and metrics contain no prohibited raw financial data
Failure injection creates no partial active financial state
Support tools obey tenant and role isolation
Destructive privacy operations are authorized, manifested, and audited
Repository completion is not represented as completed external pilots
acceptance: Non-production role and cross-tenant matrices pass
Deletion and data-export workflows produce manifests
Failure runbooks are exercised with fixtures
Load and concurrency tests stay within documented budgets
external_gates: Five in-scope pilot businesses and one or two accounting partners
Three consecutive close cycles with measured outcomes
Security and privacy review plus incident-response rehearsal

phases.name: Controlled Autonomy
phases.objective: Allow narrow low-risk transaction classes to auto-approve only after verified history, shadow evidence, consent, and revocation controls.
slices: Entity and transaction-class automation policy persistence
Eligibility engine and shadow-mode outcome comparison
Controlled activation, limits, drift monitoring, suspension, rollback, and kill switches
preflight: Verify Phase 8 repository implementation on main
Run all validators and tests
Create autonomy, eligibility, shadow-mode, incident, and go-no-go documents
hard_invariants: There is no global autopilot; autonomy is per entity and transaction class
High-risk owner, personal, loan, asset, tax, statutory, related-party, and complex-FX classes are never eligible
Every automated decision records policy, evidence, eligibility, consent, and version history
Drift, conflicts, correction spikes, or policy changes suspend automation
A global and entity-level kill switch remains available
acceptance: Eligibility is deterministic, explainable, and testable
Shadow mode produces no accounting mutation
Revocation stops future automation without deleting history
Kill switches and drift suspension pass failure tests
external_gates: Successful Phase 8 pilot evidence
Reviewed shadow-mode sample and precision thresholds
Explicit entity consent and accountant approval
Security, incident, and rollback rehearsal
